sections.localeHint: Language, digit shaping, and calendar system. Each is independent — Arabic locale can use Western digits and the Gregorian calendar if you prefer.

a11yReport.title: Accessibility report
a11yReport.subtitle: Latest CI axe-core results across every screen × locale × brand × theme.
a11yReport.missing: No report has been generated yet. Run the a11y suite to populate this page.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.subtitle: How Diwan is built and tested for inclusive use.

standard.title: Standard
standard.body: Diwan targets WCAG 2.1 Level AA across every screen, both locales (Arabic + English), both brand skins, and both light/dark themes. Logical CSS properties are used throughout so the interface mirrors correctly under right-to-left direction.

bullets.locales: Arabic (ar-SA) and English (en) — full UI parity, including Hijri (Umm al-Qura) and Gregorian calendars and Arabic-Indic / Western numeral systems.
bullets.themes: Light, dark, and system-tracking themes — color contrast verified at WCAG AA on each.
bullets.brands: Bayan and Alt brand tokens — verified independently because color contrast can vary per skin.
bullets.viewports: Mobile (≥360 px), tablet (≥768 px), and laptop (≥1280 px). Every interactive element is at least 44×44 CSS px on touch targets.

automated.title: Automated checks
automated.body: Every pull request runs axe-core against every screen × locale × brand × theme combination. Zero critical or serious violations is a merge gate, not advisory. Visual regression snapshots cover the same matrix at mobile and laptop viewports.

manual.title: Manual screen-reader passes
manual.orca: Orca on Linux is the primary manual target — recorded against the Transactions grid in Arabic before each release.
manual.voiceover: VoiceOver on macOS is a secondary check when a Mac is available.
manual.nvda: NVDA on Windows is out of scope for this artifact — the developer environment is Linux.

knownGaps.title: Known gaps
knownGaps.body: Recharts SVG charts expose data via accessible labels but not as a navigable data table; users who prefer keyboard exploration of chart points should switch to the Transactions screen, which exposes the same data as an ARIA grid.

contact.title: Reporting issues
contact.body: Open an issue on the GitHub repository if you encounter an accessibility barrier.

agent.title: Agent
agent.subtitle: Ask in Arabic or English. The agent translates your request into a transactions filter.
agent.emptyHint: Try: "failed top-ups in Riyadh over 100 SAR last week".
